ships.name: Ant
ships.description: With the recent uptick in Unfettered raids in the north of Hai space, a new model of ship, the Ant, has become increasingly popular. The Ant is a modified Aphid that uses semi-automated systems to allow one person to command a defensible merchant ship, a must for traders in an increasingly violent galaxy.

ships.name: Barquentine
ships.description: Originally designed as a flagship for the Free Worlds' war fleet, the Barquentine is a unique model based off the Southbound Shipyards Dreadnought. Armed with no fewer than 16 gun ports, the Barquentine is capable of taking down a Navy Carrier - and its full fighter and drone complement - in the hands of a skilled pilot. However, its mass imbalance and broadside weapon mounting makes it the most difficult ship to pilot in all of human space.

ships.name: Catamaran
ships.description: While the Shuttle is a perennial classic among young pilots seeking to make money without hauling cargo or risking their lives, there are few affordable improvements over it. Seeing this, a young upstart company called Tomcat Shipping cut a Shuttle in half and bolted it to a mangled Clipper. The resultant product is the Tomcat Catamaran, the most versatile transport of its class!

ships.name: Caterpillar
ships.description: While pleasure craft are very popular among citizens of the Paradise Planets, they often need to be delivered or transported en masse. For this, the Syndicate hired Tomcat Shipping to turn the existing Bulk Freighter design into a carrier. The resultant ship is lighter than the original and is capable of carrying no fewer than twelve pleasure craft, plus a little bit of extra cargo on the side.

ships.name: Eagle
ships.description: Ever since the Tarazed Falcon was released, starship captains across the galaxy have argued over whether it or the Betelgeuse Leviathan is a better ship. After some arguments got out of hand, Greenrock Shipyards, Ltd. produced a new ship from the parts of the Falcon and Leviathan, complete with a big cannon in the front. Unfortunately, shoving two hulls together tends to produce extra drag, so the Eagle will forever be remembered as a compromise ship: neither fast, nor particularly strong.

ships.name: Grackle
ships.description: The Grackle is a popular ship for those wishing to board and capture pirate vessels. It can defend itself well, while still having space for a lot of passengers or crew, and it also is a formidable light warship in its own right. It is produced by the Sirius Pyrotechnics Corporation, a company that exists for the sole purpose of providing demented starships to customers.

ships.name: Hare
ships.description: When explorers and couriers in the galactic east despaired of the choice between a Scout and a Bounder, a small startup stepped in and made the decision even harder. The Hare is the result, a Bounder-Scout hybrid that has abnormally high cargo and outfit capacity for its size and can be customized in any number of ways.

ships.name: Hornet
ships.description: Although both the Wasp and the Barb are made by the Syndicate, their true potential remained unrealized until an engineer accidentally glued a pair of scale models together. If the Barb and Wasp are considered ugly by most captains, the Hornet is considered a monstrous aberration. However, it is capable of matching a Hawk or Berserker in combat, and can mount a heavy turret or four lighter guns.

ships.name: Khosakaska
ships.description: Without the pure speed of a Veusade or the hull strength of a medium warship, the Khosakaska is designed to serve as the centre of a patrol group. The Khosokaska's versatility places it in a strong position relative to the venerable Veusade, and it is increasingly becoming the backbone of High Houses' fleets.

ships.name: Missile Frigate
ships.description: For centuries, the Frigate was the mainstay of the Republic Navy. However, recent combat against the Free Worlds showed it to be a fragile design unsuited to the modern era. A number of bright young minds in the Navy have thus seen fit to experiment with the classic design, aiming to produce a design with enough firepower to take down any other ship of its class in one rapid assault.

ships.name: Model 2
ships.description: The Model 2 is the very earliest stage of a spacefaring Kor Mereti automaton. These tiny ships, incapable of even controlling their own motion, are ejected into space regularly from Kor Mereti planets, and made to fend for themselves. Few survive, but those that do grow in the depths of space, eventually gaining the ability to travel through hyperspace.

ships.name: Model 192
ships.description: While Mereti automata usually progress perfectly fine in their evolution, there are occasionally errors that can compound insanely quickly and produce some very war drones, nothing like the normal progression. These ships tend to be inefficient and weak for their size, but also mostly hollow, allowing them to be unusually light. For some unknown reason, one of these aberrations, the Model 192, is far more common than all the rest.

ships.name: Moissanite
ships.description: Recently, some enterprising Gegno have begun to experiment with traditional ship designs. One of the results has been the Moissanite, a large mining platform based off of the hulls of the Conglomerate and Corundum. The Moissanite is slow and cumbersome, but is capable of mounting twin Excavator Drills, making it a mining fleet in and of itself.

ships.name: Odzutsu
ships.description: The Odzutsu is the Bunrodea's ultimate weapon for large-scale combat. Typically constructed from parts of existing Ararebo and Chigiriki hulls in times of need, this carrier can carry twice as many fighters as any other Bunrodea ship and support them with an impressive array of Locust Blasters. However, if the Odzutsu gets too close to the main battle, its ponderous bulk and relatively weak defenses make it an easy target for enemy gunships.

ships.name: Rawalpindi
ships.description: The Greenrock Shipyards Rawalpindi is the most heavily armed freighter money can buy. Rumour is that it's even used by some pirates as a raiding ship. It is named after the HMS Rawalpindi, a legendary armed merchant cruiser that was destroyed on Earth more than a thousand years ago. Although naming something after a destroyed ship does not seem like the best business model, you are assured that this decision has very little impact on sales as most people struggle to pronounce the name, let alone know of its namesake.

ships.name: Trident
ships.description: After the recent success of the Sunder mining carrier, Syndicated Shipyards has decided to expand the Sunder family to include a freighter that can be built on the same assembly line. The resulting ship, dubbed the Trident, is stronger and more capacious than the Sunder, though it does not carry drones. Slightly less well-armed than other ships of a similar class, it is nonetheless quickly becoming popular among merchants due to its blazing speed - a trait usually lacking in freighters.

ships.name: Star General
ships.description: Recently, the Republic has had increasingly difficult troubles with pirates on the fringes of human space. Rather than increase funding to the Navy, policy-makers instead decided to fund a new class of ship, assembled out of Star Queen and Rainmaker parts. The Star General is the result: it is slightly less agile than the Star Queen and has slightly less capacity, but is more heavily armoured and able to easily fend off small numbers of pirates with its impressive array of missile launchers. The Republic uses the Star General as a secure luxury transport, and it is also made available to a handful of individuals that have earned the Republic's trust.

ships.name: Sunthief
ships.description: There is an ancient legend on Earth of a raven who went and stole the sun from an infinite set of nested boxes, thereby freeing the world from darkness. For many young captains in the north of human space who can afford the credits or bloodshed required to own one, the Sunthief plays the same role. A model dating back to soon after the Raven was first released to the market, this ship combines the Firebird's ruggedness with the Raven's unusual efficiency.

ships.name: Woodchip
ships.description: The Woodchip, designed by engineers working on Canyon, is an absolutely horrible ship. Although it is reasonably powerful, it is exorbitantly expensive, painfully slow-moving, and has been considered a crime against humanity because each Woodchip contains a jump drive which could have been used in the war against the Pug. The only redeeming feature of this ship - and its main selling point - is the jump drive at its core, taken along with the rest of the raiding ship that went into making it. After some deliberation, the PR manager of the firm that manufactured these ships demanded that the extra engine lobes get removed, and he got his way before being summarily dismissed the day before the company went bankrupt from legal fees.

ships.name: Flivver Lo-V
ships.description: The popularity of the Flivver has led to Lionheart producing a special version called the Flivver Lo-V. This model is not capable of hyperspace travel, nor is it particularly strong, but it is incredibly fast, and a favourite for intrastellar day-trippers. The outfit scanner that comes in the place of a shield generator is enjoyed by rich children all over the galaxy, although it is also often a pain for law enforcement.

ships.name: Hyperlight Shuttle
ships.description: The Betelgeuse Shipyards Shuttle has always been popular, and recently they have come out with a new variant on the classic. Although the Hyperlight Shuttle lacks a hyperdrive, it is extraordinarily fast due to its polished hull and atomic engines, and an excellent new take on a traditional favourite.

ships.name: Partisan
ships.description: When the Navy retires old fighters, they occasionally are sold on the civilian market as pleasure craft. These modified Lances, known as "Partisans," are not able to carry passengers like other pleasure craft, nor can they mount guns like a traditional Lance. Paired with the removal of some of their armour plating and the addition of the latest atomic engines, this makes them exceptionally manueverable - albeit not very survivable in combat.

ships.name: Modified Eagle 🇲
ships.description: Ever since the Tarazed Falcon was released, starship captains across the galaxy have argued over whether it or the Betelgeuse Leviathan is a better ship. After some arguments got out of hand, Greenrock Shipyards, Ltd. produced a new ship from the parts of the Falcon and Leviathan, complete with a big cannon in the front. Most of the time, the cannon is just a normal gun, but with some modifications, it can be used as a spinal mount. This ship has those modifications, along with some other ones.

ships.name: Ant 🇲
ships.description: With the recent uptick in Unfettered raids in the north of Hai space, a new model of ship, the Ant, has become increasingly popular. However, its mass imbalance and broadside weapon mounting makes it the most difficult ship to pilot in all of human space.

A model dating back to soon after the Raven was first released to the market, this ship combines the Firebird's ruggedness with the Raven's unusual efficiency.

ships.name: Voyager Drone 🇲
ships.description: The direct precursor to the Syndicate's modern Mining Drone, the Voyager Drone was originally intended as an autonomous science platform. With enhanced scanning capabilities and reliable built-in solar panels, the model proved invaluable not only for researchers but also for local governments looking to enhance their security operations. By the time mass-production of the model ceased, it was being used for everything from civilian escort duty to private surveillance. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

ships.name: Woodchip 🇲
ships.description: The Woodchip, designed by engineers working on Canyon, is an absolutely horrible ship. Although it is reasonably powerful, it is exorbitantly expensive, painfully slow-moving, and has been considered a crime against humanity because each Woodchip contains a jump drive which could have been used in the war against the Pug. Armed with no fewer than 16 gun ports, the Barquentine is capable of taking down a Navy Carrier - and its full fighter and drone complement - in the hands of a skilled pilot. However, its mass imbalance and broadside weapon mounting makes it the most difficult ship to pilot in all of human space.

variants.name: Barquentine 🇲 (Nuke)
variants.description: Originally designed as a flagship for the Free Worlds' war fleet, the Barquentine is a unique model based off the Southbound Shipyards Dreadnought. Armed with no fewer than 16 gun ports, the Barquentine is capable of taking down a Navy Carrier - and its full fighter and drone complement - in the hands of a skilled pilot. However, its mass imbalance and broadside weapon mounting makes it the most difficult ship to pilot in all of human space.

variants.name: Hare 🇲 (Miner)
variants.description: When explorers and couriers in the galactic east despaired of the choice between a Scout and a Bounder, a small startup stepped in and made the decision even harder. The Hare is the result, a Bounder-Scout hybrid that has abnormally high cargo and outfit capacity for its size and can be customized in any number of ways.

variants.name: Hare 🇲 (Long Haul)
variants.description: When explorers and couriers in the galactic east despaired of the choice between a Scout and a Bounder, a small startup stepped in and made the decision even harder. The Hare is the result, a Bounder-Scout hybrid that has abnormally high cargo and outfit capacity for its size and can be customized in any number of ways.

variants.name: Sunder (Patrol)
variants.description: The Sunder is a versatile mining platform equipped with dedicated prospecting equipment, most notably an undercarriage that supports two Mining Drones. The lineage of this ship can be traced back to the old asteroid mining ships used in the Sol Main Belt centuries ago; however, because of recent Syndicate efforts to further exploit the Core's mineral wealth, the newest model of the Sunder is now manufactured by Syndicated Shipyards.
